al: Albania is a southeastern European country on the Adriatic and Ionian coasts, known for its beaches, medieval architecture, and UNESCO-listed ancient cities like Berat and Gjirokaster.
ad: Andorra is a tiny principality nestled in the Pyrenees mountains between France and Spain, famous for its ski resorts and duty-free shopping.
at: Austria is a Central European country known for its Alpine scenery, imperial palaces, and rich musical heritage as the birthplace of Mozart and Strauss.
by: Belarus is a landlocked Eastern European country known for its vast forests, Soviet-era architecture, and Belovezhskaya Pushcha National Park, one of Europe\'s last primeval forests.
be: Belgium is a Western European country and the de facto capital of the European Union, renowned for its medieval town centers, Renaissance architecture, chocolate, and beer.
ba: Bosnia and Herzegovina is a Balkan country known for its Ottoman and Austro-Hungarian heritage, iconic Stari Most bridge in Mostar, and stunning mountain landscapes.
bg: Bulgaria is a Balkan nation with diverse terrain encompassing Black Sea coastline, rivers, and a mountainous interior with ski resorts and ancient Thracian ruins.
hr: Croatia is a Central European and Mediterranean country famous for its stunning Adriatic coastline, over a thousand islands, and the walled medieval city of Dubrovnik.
cy: Cyprus is an island nation in the eastern Mediterranean known for its archaeological sites, beaches, and as a crossroads of Greek, Turkish, and Middle Eastern cultures.
cz: The Czech Republic is a Central European country known for its ornate castles, historic Prague Old Town, native beers, and long history of Bohemian culture.
dk: Denmark is a Scandinavian country comprising the Jutland Peninsula and numerous islands, known for its design, cycling culture, and the concept of "hygge."
ee: Estonia is a Baltic country known for its medieval Tallinn Old Town, digital innovation as one of the world\'s most advanced digital societies, and vast forested landscapes.
fi: Finland is a Nordic country with thousands of lakes, vast boreal forests, and the northern Lapland region where the aurora borealis can be seen.
fr: France is Western Europe\'s largest country, renowned for its art, fashion, gastronomy, wine, and iconic landmarks like the Eiffel Tower and the French Riviera.
de: Germany is a Central European country and the EU\'s most populous nation, known for its engineering, automotive industry, historic castles, and Oktoberfest celebrations.
gr: Greece is a southeastern European country known as the cradle of Western civilization, famous for its ancient ruins, thousands of islands, and Mediterranean cuisine.
hu: Hungary is a landlocked Central European country known for its capital Budapest, divided by the Danube River, its thermal baths, and rich folk traditions.
is: Iceland is a Nordic island nation defined by its dramatic volcanic landscape, geysers, hot springs, glaciers, and the Northern Lights.
ie: Ireland is an island in the North Atlantic known for its lush green countryside, rich literary heritage, Celtic culture, and vibrant pub scene.
it: Italy is a southern European country known for its Renaissance art, ancient Roman ruins, world-famous cuisine, fashion, and iconic landmarks from Venice to the Amalfi Coast.
xk: Kosovo is a partially recognized state in southeastern Europe, known for its Ottoman-era architecture, medieval monasteries, and the Rugova Canyon.
lv: Latvia is a Baltic country known for its wide beaches, extensive forests, and the Art Nouveau architecture of its capital Riga, a UNESCO World Heritage site.
li: Liechtenstein is a tiny, doubly landlocked Alpine principality between Switzerland and Austria, known for its medieval castles and Alpine scenery.
lt: Lithuania is the southernmost Baltic country, known for its medieval Vilnius Old Town, the Curonian Spit sand dunes, and the Hill of Crosses pilgrimage site.
lu: Luxembourg is a small, landlocked Western European country and one of the world\'s wealthiest nations, known for its fortified medieval old quarter and major EU institutions.
mt: Malta is a small Mediterranean archipelago between Sicily and North Africa, known for its historic temples, fortified cities built by the Knights of St. John, and crystal-clear waters.
md: Moldova is a landlocked Eastern European country known for its wine regions, Orthodox monasteries, and a mix of Romanian and Soviet cultural influences.
mc: Monaco is the world\'s second-smallest country, a glamorous city-state on the French Riviera known for its casino, yacht-filled harbor, and Formula 1 Grand Prix.
me: Montenegro is a Balkan country with a rugged Adriatic coastline, medieval villages, and the stunning Bay of Kotor often compared to Norwegian fjords.
nl: The Netherlands is a low-lying Western European country known for its flat landscape of canals, tulip fields, windmills, and cycling routes, as well as its Golden Age art.
mk: North Macedonia is a landlocked Balkan country known for its ancient and Ottoman-era landmarks, Lake Ohrid, and mountainous terrain.
no: Norway is a Scandinavian country renowned for its dramatic fjords, Northern Lights, midnight sun, and as one of the world\'s leading oil and gas exporters.
pl: Poland is a Central European country with a rich history, known for its medieval architecture, Auschwitz memorial, the Tatra Mountains, and vibrant cities like Krakow.
pt: Portugal is a southern European country on the Iberian Peninsula, known for its Atlantic coastline, port wine, fado music, and historic Age of Exploration heritage.
ro: Romania is a southeastern European country known for the forested region of Transylvania, medieval castles including Bran Castle, and the painted monasteries of Bucovina.
ru: Russia is the world\'s largest country by area, spanning Eastern Europe and northern Asia, known for its varied landscapes from tundra to subtropical beaches, and landmarks like the Kremlin and Lake Baikal.
sm: San Marino is one of the world\'s smallest and oldest republics, an enclave surrounded by Italy, perched on the slopes of Monte Titano.
rs: Serbia is a landlocked Balkan country known for its lively capital Belgrade at the confluence of the Danube and Sava rivers, medieval monasteries, and vibrant nightlife.
sk: Slovakia is a Central European country known for its dramatic natural scenery, numerous castles, cave systems, and the High Tatras mountain range.
si: Slovenia is a Central European country known for its mountains, ski resorts, lakes — especially Lake Bled — and extensive cave systems including Postojna Cave.
es: Spain occupies most of the Iberian Peninsula and is known for its diverse culture, flamenco, historic cities, Mediterranean beaches, and world-renowned cuisine.
se: Sweden is a Scandinavian country known for its vast forests, coastal islands, design culture, and innovations from IKEA to Spotify.
ch: Switzerland is a mountainous Central European country known for its Alps, neutrality, banking sector, luxury watches, chocolate, and multilingual culture.
ua: Ukraine is the largest country entirely within Europe, known for its Orthodox churches, Black Sea coastline, and the fertile agricultural plains of its steppe region.
gb: The United Kingdom comprises England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland, known for its historic landmarks, cultural influence, and as the birthplace of the Industrial Revolution.
va: Vatican City is the world\'s smallest independent state, the spiritual center of the Roman Catholic Church, and home to St. Peter\'s Basilica and the Sistine Chapel.
af: Afghanistan is a landlocked country at the crossroads of Central and South Asia, known for its rugged mountainous terrain including the Hindu Kush range.
am: Armenia is a mountainous Caucasus country and one of the earliest Christian civilizations, known for its ancient monasteries, Mount Ararat views, and rich cultural heritage.
az: Azerbaijan is a Caucasus country on the Caspian Sea, known for its futuristic Baku skyline, ancient Zoroastrian fire temples, and petroleum industry.
bh: Bahrain is a small island kingdom in the Persian Gulf, known as a banking hub and for its ancient Dilmun civilization, pearl diving heritage, and Formula 1 circuit.
bd: Bangladesh is a South Asian country defined by its lush greenery and many waterways, home to the Sundarbans mangrove forest, the world\'s largest river delta.
bt: Bhutan is a small Himalayan kingdom known for measuring Gross National Happiness, its Buddhist monasteries, dramatic dzong fortresses, and pristine mountain landscapes.
bn: Brunei is a small, wealthy sultanate on the island of Borneo, known for its oil and gas wealth, opulent mosques, and tropical rainforests.
kh: Cambodia is a Southeast Asian country known for the vast Angkor Wat temple complex, one of the most important archaeological sites in the world.
cn: China is the world\'s most populous country and second-largest by area, known for the Great Wall, its rapid economic transformation, and thousands of years of continuous civilization.
ge: Georgia is a Caucasus country at the intersection of Europe and Asia, known for its ancient wine-making tradition, mountain villages, and medieval churches.
in: India is the world\'s most populous country, known for its immense diversity of languages, religions, and landscapes, from the Himalayas to tropical coastlines, and landmarks like the Taj Mahal.
id: Indonesia is the world\'s largest archipelago with over 17,000 islands, known for its beaches, volcanoes, Komodo dragons, and diverse cultures across Bali, Java, and Sumatra.
ir: Iran is a Middle Eastern country with one of the world\'s oldest civilizations, known for its Persian heritage, elaborate mosques, ancient ruins of Persepolis, and diverse landscapes.
iq: Iraq is a Western Asian country in the historic Mesopotamia region between the Tigris and Euphrates rivers, often called the "Cradle of Civilization."
il: Israel is a Middle Eastern country on the Mediterranean coast, known for its significant religious sites sacred to Judaism, Christianity, and Islam, and its high-tech economy.
jp: Japan is an East Asian island nation known for its blend of ancient traditions and cutting-edge technology, from historic temples and Mount Fuji to bullet trains and anime.
jo: Jordan is a Middle Eastern country known for the ancient rose-red city of Petra, the Dead Sea — the lowest point on Earth — and vast Wadi Rum desert.
kz: Kazakhstan is the world\'s largest landlocked country, spanning Central Asia with vast steppes, the futuristic capital Astana, and the Baikonur Cosmodrome space launch facility.
kw: Kuwait is a small, oil-rich emirate on the Persian Gulf, known for its modern skyline, Kuwait Towers, and one of the world\'s highest per-capita incomes.
kg: Kyrgyzstan is a mountainous Central Asian country on the Silk Road, known for its nomadic heritage, epic alpine lakes like Issyk-Kul, and rugged Tien Shan peaks.
la: Laos is a landlocked Southeast Asian country known for its mountainous terrain, Buddhist monasteries, French colonial architecture, and the Mekong River running along its border.
lb: Lebanon is a small Mediterranean country known for its ancient Phoenician heritage, the Roman ruins of Baalbek, cedar trees, and vibrant Beirut nightlife and cuisine.
my: Malaysia is a Southeast Asian country split between the Malay Peninsula and Borneo, known for its Petronas Twin Towers, rainforests, beaches, and multicultural cuisine.
mv: The Maldives is a tropical archipelago of over 1,000 coral islands in the Indian Ocean, renowned for luxury overwater resorts, turquoise lagoons, and world-class diving.
mn: Mongolia is a vast, landlocked country between China and Russia, known for its nomadic culture, the Gobi Desert, rolling steppes, and the legacy of Genghis Khan.
mm: Myanmar is a Southeast Asian country known for its thousands of ancient Buddhist temples in Bagan, golden Shwedagon Pagoda, and diverse ethnic groups.
np: Nepal is a Himalayan country home to eight of the world\'s ten tallest mountains including Mount Everest, known for trekking, ancient temples, and rich cultural traditions.
kp: North Korea is one of the world\'s most isolated countries, occupying the northern half of the Korean Peninsula with a centrally planned economy and limited foreign access.
om: Oman is an Arabian Peninsula country known for its dramatic desert landscapes, ancient forts, frankincense trade heritage, and stunning fjord-like Musandam Peninsula.
pk: Pakistan is a South Asian country with diverse geography from the Karakoram mountains and K2 to the Arabian Sea coast, known for its Mughal architecture and ancient Indus Valley sites.
ps: Palestine encompasses the West Bank and Gaza Strip, home to significant historical and religious sites including Bethlehem and parts of the ancient city of Hebron.
ph: The Philippines is a Southeast Asian archipelago of over 7,600 islands, known for its tropical beaches, coral reefs, rice terraces, and vibrant festival culture.
qa: Qatar is a small, wealthy Persian Gulf peninsula known for its futuristic Doha skyline, vast natural gas reserves, and hosting the 2022 FIFA World Cup.
sa: Saudi Arabia is the largest country in the Middle East, home to Islam\'s two holiest cities Mecca and Medina, and one of the world\'s leading oil producers.
sg: Singapore is a densely populated city-state in Southeast Asia, known as a global financial hub with a multicultural population, iconic Marina Bay Sands, and world-class street food.
kr: South Korea is an East Asian country known for its pop culture phenomenon of K-pop, advanced technology, Buddhist temples, and bustling capital Seoul.
lk: Sri Lanka is an island nation in the Indian Ocean known for its ancient Buddhist ruins, diverse landscapes from rainforest to arid plains, and renowned Ceylon tea.
sy: Syria is a Western Asian country with Damascus, one of the world\'s oldest continuously inhabited cities, and a rich history spanning ancient civilizations and Crusader-era castles.
tw: Taiwan is an East Asian island known for its modern cities, traditional Chinese temples, hot springs, and dramatic mountainous terrain including Taroko Gorge.
tj: Tajikistan is a mountainous Central Asian country where over 90% of the land is mountains, home to the towering Pamir range known as the "Roof of the World."
th: Thailand is a Southeast Asian country known for its tropical beaches, ornate Buddhist temples, royal palaces, and world-famous street food and cuisine.
tl: Timor-Leste is one of the world\'s youngest nations, occupying the eastern half of Timor island, known for its coral reefs, mountainous interior, and Portuguese colonial heritage.
tr: Turkey straddles Europe and Asia across the Bosphorus strait, known for its rich history from the Byzantine and Ottoman empires, iconic mosques, and diverse landscapes.
tm: Turkmenistan is a Central Asian country largely covered by the Karakum Desert, known for its marble-clad capital Ashgabat, ancient Silk Road ruins, and the flaming Darvaza gas crater.
ae: The UAE is a federation of seven emirates on the Arabian Peninsula, known for the ultramodern cities of Dubai and Abu Dhabi, luxury shopping, and ambitious architecture.
uz: Uzbekistan is a Central Asian country on the ancient Silk Road, known for the stunning Islamic architecture of Samarkand, Bukhara, and Khiva.
vn: Vietnam is a Southeast Asian country known for its beaches, rivers, Buddhist pagodas, and vibrant cities, as well as Ha Long Bay\'s limestone karst formations.
ye: Yemen is a country at the southern tip of the Arabian Peninsula, known for its ancient Old City of Sana\'a, the island of Socotra, and historic frankincense and myrrh trade.
dz: Algeria is Africa\'s largest country by area, mostly covered by the Sahara Desert, with a Mediterranean coastline and Roman ruins like Timgad and Djemila.
ao: Angola is a Southern African country with a long Atlantic coastline, known for its oil and diamond resources, diverse wildlife, and Portuguese colonial heritage.
bj: Benin is a West African country and the birthplace of the Vodun (Voodoo) religion, known for its palm-lined beaches and the historic Kingdom of Dahomey.
bw: Botswana is a landlocked Southern African country known for the Okavango Delta, one of the world\'s largest inland deltas, and its thriving diamond mining industry.
bf: Burkina Faso is a landlocked West African country known for its vibrant arts scene, traditional music, and the FESPACO pan-African film festival.
bi: Burundi is a small, densely populated Central African country near the Great Rift Valley, known for its hilly terrain, Lake Tanganyika shoreline, and traditional drumming.
cv: Cape Verde is a volcanic archipelago off West Africa known for its Creole culture, dramatic landscapes, and music genre morna, popularized by singer Cesaria Evora.
cm: Cameroon is often called "Africa in miniature" for its geological and cultural diversity, with beaches, deserts, mountains, rainforests, and savannas.
cf: The Central African Republic is a landlocked country in central Africa, known for its rainforests, diverse wildlife, and the Dzanga-Sangha reserve with forest elephants and gorillas.
td: Chad is a large landlocked Central African country spanning the Sahara Desert in the north and fertile savannas in the south, with Lake Chad at its western border.
km: Comoros is a volcanic archipelago off southeastern Africa in the Mozambique Channel, known for its aromatic ylang-ylang and vanilla production and rich marine biodiversity.
cg: The Republic of the Congo is a Central African country with vast tropical rainforests, known for its wildlife including lowland gorillas and forest elephants.
cd: The Democratic Republic of the Congo is Africa\'s second-largest country, home to the Congo River basin rainforest, mountain gorillas, and enormous mineral wealth.
ci: Ivory Coast is a West African country and the world\'s largest cocoa producer, known for its French colonial legacy, diverse landscapes, and the Basilica of Yamoussoukro.
dj: Djibouti is a small country in the Horn of Africa at the southern entrance to the Red Sea, known for Lake Assal — Africa\'s lowest point — and its strategic port.
eg: Egypt is a transcontinental country linking northeast Africa with the Middle East, known for its ancient civilization, the Pyramids of Giza, the Sphinx, and the Nile River.
gq: Equatorial Guinea is one of the smallest and wealthiest countries in Africa, located on the west coast with significant oil reserves and Spanish colonial heritage.
er: Eritrea is a Horn of Africa country on the Red Sea coast, known for its Art Deco capital Asmara, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and the Dahlak Archipelago.
sz: Eswatini (formerly Swaziland) is a small landlocked Southern African monarchy known for its game reserves, traditional Swazi culture, and the annual Reed Dance ceremony.
et: Ethiopia is Africa\'s second most populous country and one of the world\'s oldest civilizations, known for its rock-hewn churches of Lalibela and as the origin of coffee.
ga: Gabon is a Central African country on the Atlantic coast, covered by dense equatorial rainforest and known for its national parks protecting gorillas, elephants, and hippos.
gm: The Gambia is mainland Africa\'s smallest country, a narrow strip along the Gambia River known for its beaches, riverside national parks, and diverse birdlife.
gh: Ghana is a West African country known for its Gold Coast heritage, Ashanti kingdom legacy, vibrant markets, and as one of Africa\'s most stable democracies.
gn: Guinea is a West African country with a coastline on the Atlantic Ocean, known for its highland regions, bauxite reserves, and as the source of the Niger River.
gw: Guinea-Bissau is a small West African country with a tropical coastline and the Bijagos Archipelago, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve with rich marine and terrestrial wildlife.
ke: Kenya is an East African country known for its iconic savanna landscapes, the Great Wildebeest Migration in the Masai Mara, and as a hub for wildlife safaris.
ls: Lesotho is a mountainous kingdom entirely surrounded by South Africa, known as the "Kingdom in the Sky" with the highest lowest point of any country in the world.
lr: Liberia is a West African country founded by freed American slaves, known for its dense tropical forests, rubber plantations, and the oldest republic in Africa.
ly: Libya is a North African country with a long Mediterranean coastline and vast Saharan interior, known for well-preserved Roman ruins at Leptis Magna and Sabratha.
mg: Madagascar is the world\'s fourth-largest island off Africa\'s southeast coast, known for its unique wildlife including lemurs, baobab trees, and extraordinary biodiversity found nowhere else.
mw: Malawi is a landlocked southeastern African country known as the "Warm Heart of Africa," dominated by Lake Malawi, one of the world\'s largest and most biodiverse lakes.
ml: Mali is a large landlocked West African country, known for the ancient city of Timbuktu, the Great Mosque of Djenne, and its rich musical heritage.
mr: Mauritania is a vast, mostly desert West African country bridging Arab North Africa and sub-Saharan Africa, known for the Saharan Richat Structure and ancient trading towns.
mu: Mauritius is an island nation in the Indian Ocean east of Madagascar, known for its beaches, lagoons, coral reefs, and as the former home of the extinct dodo bird.
ma: Morocco is a North African country bordering the Atlantic and Mediterranean, known for its Berber, Arab, and French cultural influences, bustling medinas, and the Sahara Desert.
mz: Mozambique is a southeastern African country with a long Indian Ocean coastline, known for its marine biodiversity, coral reefs, and the Bazaruto Archipelago.
na: Namibia is a Southern African country with the ancient Namib Desert along its Atlantic coast, known for Sossusvlei\'s towering red sand dunes and diverse wildlife.
ne: Niger is a large, landlocked West African country largely covered by the Sahara Desert, known for the ancient caravan city of Agadez and its Tuareg culture.
ng: Nigeria is Africa\'s most populous country, known for its Nollywood film industry, diverse ethnic groups, oil wealth, and vibrant cities like Lagos.
rw: Rwanda is a small, hilly Central African country known as the "Land of a Thousand Hills," celebrated for its mountain gorilla conservation and remarkable post-1994 transformation.
st: São Tomé and Príncipe is a small island nation in the Gulf of Guinea, known for its volcanic peaks, cocoa plantations, and as one of Africa\'s smallest countries.
sn: Senegal is a West African country known for its vibrant culture, the historic Gorée Island slave trade site, and as a stable democracy with rich musical traditions.
sc: Seychelles is an Indian Ocean archipelago of 115 islands, known for its pristine beaches, giant Aldabra tortoises, and the unique Vallée de Mai palm forest.
sl: Sierra Leone is a West African country with a coastline on the Atlantic, known for its white-sand beaches, the Tacugama Chimpanzee Sanctuary, and diamond mining.
so: Somalia is a Horn of Africa country with the continent\'s longest coastline, known for its ancient port cities, nomadic culture, and diverse marine life.
za: South Africa is the continent\'s southernmost country, known for its varied topography, Table Mountain, Kruger National Park, and diverse multicultural society.
ss: South Sudan is the world\'s youngest country, having gained independence in 2011, known for the vast Sudd wetland, one of the largest freshwater ecosystems in the world.
sd: Sudan is a large northeastern African country where the Blue and White Nile rivers converge at Khartoum, known for its ancient Nubian pyramids and diverse landscapes.
tz: Tanzania is an East African country home to Mount Kilimanjaro — Africa\'s highest peak — the Serengeti plains, and the tropical islands of Zanzibar.
tg: Togo is a narrow West African country between Ghana and Benin, known for its palm-lined beaches, hilltop villages, and the Koutammakou UNESCO World Heritage landscape.
tn: Tunisia is the northernmost African country, known for the ancient ruins of Carthage, Saharan desert oases, Mediterranean beaches, and its Star Wars filming locations.
ug: Uganda is a landlocked East African country known as the "Pearl of Africa," home to mountain gorillas, the source of the Nile, and Lake Victoria.
zm: Zambia is a landlocked Southern African country known for Victoria Falls on its border with Zimbabwe — one of the world\'s largest and most spectacular waterfalls.
zw: Zimbabwe is a landlocked Southern African country known for its dramatic landscapes, Victoria Falls, Great Zimbabwe stone ruins, and abundant wildlife in Hwange National Park.
ag: Antigua and Barbuda is a Caribbean twin-island nation known for its 365 beaches — one for each day of the year — coral reefs, and historic Nelson\'s Dockyard.
bs: The Bahamas is an archipelago of over 700 islands in the Atlantic Ocean, known for its crystal-clear turquoise waters, luxury resorts, and the famous swimming pigs of Exuma.
bb: Barbados is an eastern Caribbean island known for its beaches, rum production, British colonial heritage, and as the birthplace of singer Rihanna.
bz: Belize is a Central American country with Caribbean coastline, known for the Great Blue Hole, the Belize Barrier Reef, and ancient Maya ruins like Caracol.
ca: Canada is the world\'s second-largest country by area, known for its vast wilderness, Rocky Mountains, multicultural cities, and maple syrup production.
cr: Costa Rica is a Central American country known for its biodiversity, rainforests, volcanoes, and beaches on both the Caribbean and Pacific coasts, and its commitment to conservation.
cu: Cuba is the Caribbean\'s largest island, known for its vintage American cars, colonial architecture, cigars, salsa music, and the historic city of Old Havana.
dm: Dominica is a mountainous Caribbean island known as the "Nature Isle of the Caribbean," with hot springs, tropical rainforest, and the Boiling Lake volcanic feature.
do: The Dominican Republic occupies the eastern two-thirds of Hispaniola, known for its beaches, resorts, golfing, and Santo Domingo — the oldest European settlement in the Americas.
sv: El Salvador is Central America\'s smallest and most densely populated country, known for its Pacific coast beaches, volcanic landscapes, and Mayan ruins at Joya de Cerén.
gd: Grenada is a Caribbean island known as the "Spice Isle" for its nutmeg and cinnamon production, with colorful hillside towns and pristine beaches.
gt: Guatemala is a Central American country known for its Maya heritage, the ancient city of Tikal, volcanic highlands, and the colorful indigenous markets of Chichicastenango.
ht: Haiti occupies the western third of Hispaniola and was the first country founded by formerly enslaved people, known for its Citadelle Laferrière and vibrant art culture.
hn: Honduras is a Central American country with Caribbean and Pacific coastlines, known for its ancient Maya ruins at Copán and the Bay Islands coral reef system.
jm: Jamaica is a Caribbean island known as the birthplace of reggae music and Bob Marley, famous for its Blue Mountains, jerk cuisine, and vibrant culture.
mx: Mexico is a large country between the U.S. and Central America, known for its ancient Mayan and Aztec ruins, vibrant cuisine, beaches, and rich cultural traditions.
ni: Nicaragua is the largest Central American country, known as the "Land of Lakes and Volcanoes" for its dramatic volcanic landscape and large freshwater lakes.
pa: Panama is a Central American country known for the Panama Canal connecting the Atlantic and Pacific oceans, its modern skyline, and biodiverse rainforests.
kn: Saint Kitts and Nevis is a two-island Caribbean nation and the smallest sovereign state in the Americas, known for its volcanic peaks and historic Brimstone Hill Fortress.
lc: Saint Lucia is a Caribbean island known for its iconic twin Pitons volcanic peaks, lush rainforests, drive-in volcano, and luxury resorts.
vc: Saint Vincent and the Grenadines is a Caribbean island chain known for its pristine beaches, the volcanic La Soufrière, and the Tobago Cays marine park.
tt: Trinidad and Tobago is a twin-island Caribbean nation known for its Carnival celebration, calypso and soca music, and the Pitch Lake — the world\'s largest natural deposit of asphalt.
us: The United States is the world\'s third-largest country by area, known for its diverse geography from the Grand Canyon to Hawaii, cultural influence, and technological innovation.
ar: Argentina is South America\'s second-largest country, known for the Andes mountains, Patagonian glaciers, tango, beef, and wine from the Mendoza region.
bo: Bolivia is a landlocked South American country known for the Salar de Uyuni — the world\'s largest salt flat — Andean highlands, and diverse indigenous cultures.
br: Brazil is South America\'s largest country, known for the Amazon rainforest, Rio de Janeiro\'s Carnival, Christ the Redeemer statue, and its passion for football.
cl: Chile is a long, narrow country along South America\'s western coast, stretching from the Atacama Desert — the world\'s driest — to Patagonian glaciers and Easter Island.
co: Colombia is a South American country known for its coffee production, Caribbean and Pacific coastlines, Andean highlands, and the colorful colonial city of Cartagena.
ec: Ecuador is a South American country on the equator, known for the Galápagos Islands with their unique wildlife, Andean highlands, and Amazon rainforest.
gy: Guyana is a South American country on the Atlantic coast, known for its dense rainforests, Kaieteur Falls — one of the world\'s tallest single-drop waterfalls — and Caribbean-influenced culture.
py: Paraguay is a landlocked South American country divided by the Paraguay River, known for its Guaraní indigenous culture, Jesuit mission ruins, and the Chaco wilderness.
pe: Peru is a South American country known for the ancient Inca citadel of Machu Picchu, the Amazon rainforest, and a world-renowned culinary scene centered in Lima.
sr: Suriname is South America\'s smallest country, known for its vast tropical rainforest covering over 90% of its territory, Dutch colonial architecture, and ethnic diversity.
uy: Uruguay is a small South American country known for its beaches along the Río de la Plata and Atlantic coast, progressive policies, and mate tea culture.
ve: Venezuela is a South American country on the Caribbean coast, known for Angel Falls — the world\'s highest uninterrupted waterfall — vast oil reserves, and the Orinoco River basin.
au: Australia is both a country and a continent, known for the Great Barrier Reef, the Outback, unique wildlife like kangaroos and koalas, and vibrant coastal cities.
fj: Fiji is a South Pacific archipelago of more than 330 islands, known for its rugged volcanic landscapes, palm-lined beaches, coral reefs, and warm Fijian hospitality.
ki: Kiribati is a Pacific island nation straddling the equator, one of the most remote countries on Earth and among the first to face the effects of rising sea levels.
mh: The Marshall Islands is a Pacific island chain of volcanic islands and coral atolls, known for the Bikini Atoll nuclear test site and world-class scuba diving.
fm: Micronesia is a Pacific island nation of over 600 islands, known for ancient stone ruins of Nan Madol, vibrant coral reefs, and diverse marine life.
nr: Nauru is the world\'s smallest island nation and third-smallest country, a single raised coral island in the Pacific once wealthy from phosphate mining.
nz: New Zealand is a Pacific island country known for its dramatic landscapes from fjords to volcanoes, Māori culture, and as the filming location for The Lord of the Rings.
pw: Palau is a Pacific archipelago of over 500 islands, known for its pristine Rock Islands, world-famous Jellyfish Lake, and some of the best diving on the planet.
pg: Papua New Guinea is one of the world\'s most culturally diverse countries, with over 800 languages spoken, dense rainforests, and tribal traditions still practiced today.
ws: Samoa is a Polynesian island country in the South Pacific, known for its traditional fa\'a Samoa culture, lush rainforests, volcanic landscapes, and the To Sua Ocean Trench.
sb: The Solomon Islands is a Pacific archipelago east of Papua New Guinea, known for its WWII battle sites, diverse marine life, and traditional Melanesian culture.
to: Tonga is a Polynesian kingdom of over 170 South Pacific islands, known for its royal heritage, whale watching, ancient stone trilithon, and traditional culture.
tv: Tuvalu is one of the world\'s smallest and most remote countries, a Polynesian island chain in the Pacific and one of the nations most vulnerable to climate change.
vu: Vanuatu is a South Pacific archipelago of roughly 80 islands, known for its active volcanoes including Mount Yasur, pristine coral reefs, and traditional bungee jumping origins.
